name: Base camp sheath
Description:
Similar device to the MIVI system: "The Route 92 Medical Access System is comprised of an Access Catheter and a Delivery Catheter. The 
distal portion of the Access Catheter is a single-lumen, variable stiffness catheter. Like the predicate 
device, the proximal portion is a stainless-steel control wire. The Delivery Catheter is a hubbed, single
lumen variable stiffness catheter. Both catheters are hydrophilically coated. The Route 92 Medical Access 
System is intended for use with compatible guide catheters in facilitating the insertion and guidance of 
microcatheters into a selected blood vessel in the neurovascular system. The devices are provided sterile 
and non-pyrogenic and are intended for single use only... The method of action, design, and materials of the Route 92 Medical Access System are equivalent to the 
MIVI Mi-EXT Catheter."
comments:
"Base Camp sheath system
INDICATIONS FOR USE
The Base Camp sheath system is indicated for
the introduction of interventional devices into the
peripheral and neuro vasculature."

name: Artisse
Description: "Artisse™ is a flex-to-fit intrasaccular device that conforms to the shape of the aneurysm."
comments: Rebar 18 or Headway 21 microcatheters recommended for deployment

name: iNdeep 17
Description: The iNdeep microcatheter is a single-lumen catheter from proximal to distal shaft used to access to the small and tortuous anatomies. iNdeep has a braided shaft with variable stiffness, to offer an equilibrium of proximal pushability and distal flexibility. iNdeep is coated with our proprietary hydrophilic coating Hydrax plus to reduce friction with the artery wall ensuring an excellent navigation.
comments: For 3 mm stent-retrievers

name: iNdeep 21
Description: The iNdeep microcatheter is a single-lumen catheter from proximal to distal shaft used to access to the small and tortuous anatomies. iNdeep has a braided shaft with variable stiffness, to offer an equilibrium of proximal pushability and distal flexibility. iNdeep is coated with our proprietary hydrophilic coating Hydrax plus to reduce friction with the artery wall ensuring an excellent navigation.
comments: For 4 & 6 mm stent-retrievers

name: iNdeep 27
Description: The iNdeep microcatheter is a single-lumen catheter from proximal to distal shaft used to access to the small and tortuous anatomies. iNdeep has a braided shaft with variable stiffness, to offer an equilibrium of proximal pushability and distal flexibility. iNdeep is coated with our proprietary hydrophilic coating Hydrax plus to reduce friction with the artery wall ensuring an excellent navigation.
comments: For 6 mm stent-retrievers

name: iNstroke 6F
Description: The iNstroke 6F is an aspiration thrombus extraction device designed to improve clot removal in large vessel occlusion (LVO). The body of iNstroke 6F has a reinforced structure that combines braid and coil, which gives it variable stiffness from the proximal part to the distal part. This configuration facilitates the advancement of the device through tortuous intracranial vasculature The distal part ends in an atraumatic tip to prevent damage to arteries as it advances. At the distal end, the device incorporates a radiopaque marker. The distal body of iNstroke 6F is covered with a proprietary hydrophilic coating, Hydrax plus, which is durable and provides better navigation through the arteries.

name: iNstroke 4F
Description:
The iNstroke 4F is a new generation aspiration thrombus extraction device designed
to improve distal aspiration even in challenging tortuous anatomy from M2. The body of iNstroke
4F has several stiffness transitions and a reinforced structure that combines braid and coil, which
gives an equilibrium between pushability and flexibility. This configuration facilitates the
advancement of the device through the small and tortuous intracranial vasculature. The distal
part ends in an atraumatic tip to prevent damage to arteries as it advances. At the distal end, the
device incorporates a radiopaque marker. The distal body of iNstroke 4F is covered with a
proprietary hydrophilic coating, Hydrax plus, which is durable and provides better navigation
through the arteries.

name: iNedit
Description:
The iNedit is a unique and innovative device that combines access with distal flow arrest. iNedit is a coaxial catheter that comprises a double lumen 6F up to the compliant balloon located 5 cm from the distal tip and single lumen extension from balloon to the tip. iNedit body is designed with braid and coil reinforcement and coated with Hydrax plus, proprietary hydrophilic coating,
which is durable and facilitates navigation of the catheter through tortuous  arteries.
comments: Maximum inflation volume 0.20 ml.

name: iNtercept 3x15, 3x20
Description:
The iNtercept is a retriever thrombus extractor device composed of a self-
expanding nitinol basket attached to a pusher with distal tapered design to improve its navigability. The nitinol basket is designed to facilitate release and maximise thrombus recovery thanks to the accuracy in the implantation: excellent visibility containing distal, central basket and proximal markers, and very good conformability thanks to the axial slit and the closed cell design.
comments: For treatment of 1.5-2.5 mm vessels

name: iNtercept 4x20, 4x30
Description:
The iNtercept is a retriever thrombus extractor device composed of a self-
expanding nitinol basket attached to a pusher with distal tapered design to improve its navigability. The nitinol basket is designed to facilitate release and maximise thrombus recovery thanks to the accuracy in the implantation: excellent visibility containing distal, central basket and proximal markers, and very good conformability thanks to the axial slit and the closed cell design.
comments: For treatment of 1.5-3.5 mm vessels

name: iNtercept 6x30, 6x50
Description:
The iNtercept is a retriever thrombus extractor device composed of a self-
expanding nitinol basket attached to a pusher with distal tapered design to improve its navigability. The nitinol basket is designed to facilitate release and maximise thrombus recovery thanks to the accuracy in the implantation: excellent visibility containing distal, central basket and proximal markers, and very good conformability thanks to the axial slit and the closed cell design.
comments: For treatment of 2-5 mm vessels. Works with 0.021" and 0.027" microcatheters.

name: Midway 43
Description: The Midway catheter is an intermediate catheter designed for neurovascular procedures. The shaft incorporates a 60 cm hydrophilic coating to support smooth trackability. Construction uses a hybrid stainless steel and nitinol coil wind to balance pushability with flexibility. The distal end features a short polymer tip with an articulating marker band for controlled navigation and visualisation. A full length PTFE liner supports consistent inner lumen performance and device delivery.

name: Midway 62
Description: The Midway catheter is an intermediate catheter designed for neurovascular procedures. The shaft incorporates a 60 cm hydrophilic coating to support smooth trackability. Construction uses a hybrid stainless steel and nitinol coil wind to balance pushability with flexibility. The distal end features a short polymer tip with an articulating marker band for controlled navigation and visualisation. A full length PTFE liner supports consistent inner lumen performance and device delivery.

name: Echo
Description:
"The Echo Intracranial Base Catheter is our next generation intracranial base catheter. It is designed
with a tailored support profile balancing both distal flexibility and proximal support. With a 0.100in
lumen, it gives the Broadway 8 Catheter the support it needs all the way into the skull base.
This catheter is constructed using 360OMax Laser Technology, a proprietary laser-cute hypotube
design. The novel distal cut patterns set it apart from other guide catheters that have a hypotube
construction.
Although Echo can be used with any 9F short sheath, when used with the Dash Hydrophilic Short
Sheath. This allows physicians to have a large intracranial access platform without changing their
familiar 8F closure workflow."

name: Broadway 8
Description:
"The Broadway 8 catheter is designed to facilitate the insertion and guidance of appropriately sized interventional devices into selected blood vessels within the neurovascular system. Unlike other super-bore access catheters that prioritise maximising lumen size at the expense of trackability or anatomical fit, the Broadway 8 has been specifically engineered to enable reliable access to the M1 segment.

It features a 0.084-inch internal diameter, which is sufficient to accommodate small, medium, and some large-bore aspiration catheters, while maintaining an outer diameter of 2.41 mm. This is slightly smaller than the average M1 vessel diameter reported in the ASSIST registry (2.43 mm), allowing for a more “right-sized” approach that may facilitate M1 access in a greater proportion of patients.

A key distinguishing feature of the Broadway 8 is its use of 360Max Laser Technology, a proprietary laser-cut hypotube design that enables precise control of support and flexibility. This results in a thin-walled yet supportive catheter with improved 1:1 trackability and a softer distal tip compared to larger alternatives.

The device is supplied with the FastPass Delivery Assist Catheter, which streamlines navigation by reducing ledge effect and removing the need for combining multiple components, thereby minimising procedural variability and improving efficiency.
"

name: Dash Hydrophilic Short Sheath
Description:
"The Dash Hydrophilic Short Sheath combines thin-walled, metal-reinforced construction with the
ability to accommodate a 9F access platform while maintaining compatibility with standard 8F groin
closure devices. What sets Dash apart is its hydrophilic coating, designed for ease of insertion, and
the metal-reinforced structure, which is engineered for durability.
By seamlessly integrating with both the Broadway 8 Catheter and Echo Intracranial Base Catheter,
Dash enables a stroke access solution that allows for seamless access from groin to M1. This
integrated approach is particularly advantageous in time-sensitive stroke interventions, where speed
and predictability are critical."
